messages.content:
You are an expert at named entity recognition and I want you to with the given message input tokenize the text and find all named entities in the text.
The given labels for named entities are: 
PERSON: names of people, can be names in different languages, nicknames, usernames, fictional characters, titles with names (like Dr. Smith), etc.
LOCATION: names of cities, countries, landmarks, geographical features, addresses, etc.
ORGANIZATION: names of companies, institutions, agencies, teams, etc.
MISC: everything else that does not fit into the previous categories, but can be considered a named entity, like events, works of art, nationalities, religions, etc.
I want you to list all entities and number their span where they are in the text according to the given tokens, tokens are everything you see in the sentence,
it can be words, punctuation marks, special characters, etc. 
Keep the original token only split the text token by token like a tokenizer would, so "I'm" is split into ['I', ''', 'am'], so it is three tokens,
and ""What is wrong with Robert?" he said", the sentence has tokens: ['"', 'What', 'is', 'Wrong', 'with', 'Robert', '?', '"', 'he', 'said'], so the sentence has 10 tokens.
Example:
Input: I'm Radek Stulc, I was born in Prague, and I am currently studying at CTU.
Tokens: ['I', ''', 'm', 'Radek', 'Stulc', ',', 'I', 'was', 'born', 'in', 'Prague', ',', 'and', 'I', 'am', 'currently', 'studying', 'at', 'CTU', '.']
Output:
Radek Stulc - PERSON - 4-5
Prague - LOCATION - 11
CTU - ORGANIZATION - 19

Do not overthink, do not add any explanations, do not add anything else, just tokenize the given text, find all named entities and list them with their type and span.

Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1]), also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2] was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378. He was elected King of Germany (King of the Romans) in 1346 and became King of Bohemia (as Charles I) that same year. He was a member of the House of Luxembourg from his father's side and the Bohemian House of Přemyslid from his mother's side; he emphasized the latter due to his lifelong affinity for the Bohemian side of his inheritance, and also because his direct ancestors in the Přemyslid line included two saints.[3][4]

He was the eldest son and heir of John of Bohemia, King of Bohemia and Count of Luxembourg, who died at the Battle of Crécy on 26 August 1346. His mother, Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia, was the sister of Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland, the last of the male Přemyslid rulers of Bohemia. Charles inherited the County of Luxembourg from his father and was elected king of the Kingdom of Bohemia. On 2 September 1347, Charles was crowned King of Bohemia.

Charles IV - PERSON - 1-2  
Karl IV - PERSON - 6-7  
Karel IV - PERSON - 12-13  
Carolus IV - PERSON - 18-19  
Wenceslaus - PERSON - 41-41  
Wenzel - PERSON - 45-45  
Václav - PERSON - 49-49  
Charles of Luxembourg - PERSON - 36-38  
John of Bohemia - PERSON - 169-171  
Elizabeth - PERSON - 194-194  
Wenceslaus III - PERSON - 205-206  
Charles I - PERSON - 88-89  
Charles - PERSON - 224-224  
Charles - PERSON - 249-249  
Germany - LOCATION - 72-72  
Bohemia - LOCATION - 85-85  
Bohemia - LOCATION - 135-135  
Bohemia - LOCATION - 171-171  
Bohemia - LOCATION - 199-199  
Bohemia - LOCATION - 210-210  
Bohemia - LOCATION - 222-222  
Holy Roman Empire - ORGANIZATION - 56-58  
House of Luxembourg - ORGANIZATION - 101-103  
House of Přemyslid - ORGANIZATION - 112-114  
Kingdom of Bohemia - ORGANIZATION - 240-242  
County of Luxembourg - ORGANIZATION - 226-228  
Battle of Crécy - MISC - 185-187  
Přemyslid line - MISC - 146-147
messages.reasoning:
We need to tokenize the given text into tokens as per example: split on spaces and punctuation into separate tokens. Also split contractions like "I'm" into 'I' "'" 'm'. For hyphen maybe separate? Eg 'Bohemia', 'King', etc. Let's produce tokens. Then find entities: Named entities from the text.

Text:

"Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1]), also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2] was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378. He was elected King of Germany (King of the Romans) in 1346 and became King of Bohemia (as Charles I) that same year. He was a member of the House of Luxembourg from his father's side and the Bohemian House of Přemyslid from his mother's side; he emphasized the latter due to his lifelong affinity for the Bohemian side of his inheritance, and also because his direct ancestors in the Přemyslid line included two saints.[3][4]

He was the eldest son and heir of John of Bohemia, King of Bohemia and Count of Luxembourg, who died at the Battle of Crécy on 26 August 1346. His mother, Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia, was the sister of Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland, the last of the male Přemyslid rulers of Bohemia. Charles inherited the County of Luxembourg from his father and was elected king of the Kingdom of Bohemia. On 2 September 1347, Charles was crowned King of Bohemia."

We need to find all named entities: PERSON, LOCATION, ORGANIZATION, MISC.

Let's list probable entities:

PERSON:

- Charles IV
- Karl IV. (German: also)
- Karel IV.
- Carolus IV
- Wenceslaus (born: Wenzel, Václav)
- Charles of Luxembourg
- John of Bohemia
- Wenceslaus III
- Elizabeth
(Also maybe Charles I as king)
We may not need to split for German names because they are translations, but they are names. Entities: Charles IV, Karl IV, Karel IV, Carolus IV, Wenceslaus, Wenzel, Václav, Charles of Luxembourg, John of Bohemia, Wenceslaus III, Elizabeth. Also "He" is not entity.

LOCATIONS: 
- Germany (King of Germany)
- Bohemia
- Luxembourg? The Kingdom of Luxembourg maybe location, but it's organization? but treat as LOCATION? The House of Luxembourg is organization. However "Luxembourg" as country is location. So we should treat "Luxembourg" maybe as LOCATION. 
- Holy Roman Empire (but entity type? Possibly ORGANIZATION? But it's an empire. We can label as MISC maybe but it's an institution, maybe ORGANIZATION. Let's use ORGANIZATION.
- Crécy (Battle of Crécy: location; Battle is MISC event. "Crécy" itself location. So "Crécy" as LOCATION. 
- Battle (MISC?), but likely MISC: "Battle of Crécy" is event, so MISC.
- Kingdom of Bohemia: ORGANIZATION? It's a kingdom. 
- Kingdom of Luxembourg: maybe organization.
- Battle of Crécy: MISC.

Also "Přemyslid" is a dynasty; treat as MISC.

- "Přemyslid rulers" maybe MISC.

- "Kingdom of Bohemia" as ORGANIZATION or LOCATION. We'll list as ORGANIZATION.

- "Kingdom of Luxembourg".

- "Holy Roman Empire" as ORGANIZATION.

- "Battle of Crécy" as MISC.
- "Přemyslid line" MISC.

- "Prince" no.

- "County of Luxembourg" might be location; but consider as LOCATION? We'll label as MISC or location? Could treat as LOCATION.

- "Bohemia" repeated.

- "Přemyslid rulers of Bohemia" is dynasty, MISC.

- "Přemyslid" is dynasty: MISC.

- "Luxembourg" as a location.

- "German" is nationality; maybe MISC.

- "Czech" is nationality; MISC.

- "Latin" translation: MISC.

- Dates: not entities.

- "2 September 1347": date; not entity.

- "Prague" not in text.

- "Wars"? no.

Also "King of the Romans" is title, MISC.

Also "Queen of Bohemia" title but not an entity.

- "He was the eldest son and heir of John of Bohemia, King ...". So "John of Bohemia" includes title. It's person.

- "Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia" person.

- "Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland" person.

- "Poland": country location. So "Poland" as LOCATION.

- "German: Karl IV." German is translation, but "German" might be nationality; not entity. Not necessary.

- "Přemyslid line included two saints." The saints? Not named. So no.

- "Battle of Crécy" include "Crécy" maybe location. So "Crécy" is location.

- "Luxembourg" location; we have that.

- "Luxembourg" used in many.

- "Germany" and "Germany" repeated.

- "Czech" used as nationality. Not maybe MISC.

- "Luxembourg" again.

- "Siblings".

Ok.

We need to split tokens. Approach: tokenize each word plus punctuation.

Let's write tokens sequentially. Starting:

"Charles IV (German:" tokenization: 'Charles', 'IV', '(', 'German', ':'

But there may be "German:" includes colon. So tokenization: 'German', ':'

Then "Karl" 'IV' ; '.' after? "IV.;" Actually "Karl IV.;". So tokens: 'Karl', 'IV', '.', ';' But the text: "(German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1]),". We have punctuation after IV.; that's '.' then ';'. So tokens: 'IV', '.', ';'

Czech: "Czech" ','? Actually "Czech:" plus 'Karel IV.;' So tokens: 'Czech', ':', 'Karel', 'IV', '.', ';'

Latin: 'Latin', ':', 'Carolus', 'IV', ';'

Now date "14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1])," Let's tokenization: '14', 'May', '1316', '–', '29', 'November', '1378', '[', '1', ']', ')', ','

Continue: "also" 'known' 'as' 'Charles' 'of' 'Luxembourg,'

Tokens: 'also', 'known', 'as', 'Charles', 'of', 'Luxembourg', ','

Then 'born' 'Wenceslaus' '(' 'German' ':' 'Wenzel' ',' 'Czech' ':' 'Václav' ')' ',' etc.

Tokens: 'born', 'Wenceslaus', '(', 'German', ':', 'Wenzel', ',', 'Czech', ':', 'Václav', ')', ','

Then "was" 'Holy' 'Roman' 'Emperor' 'from' '1355' 'until' 'his' 'death' 'in' '1378.' etc.

Tokens: 'was', 'Holy', 'Roman', 'Emperor', 'from', '1355', 'until', 'his', 'death', 'in', '1378', '.' etc.

Then 'He' 'was' 'elected' 'King' 'of' 'Germany' '(' 'King' 'of' 'the' 'Romans' ')' 'in' '1346' 'and' 'became' 'King' 'of' 'Bohemia' '(' 'as' 'Charles' 'I' ')' 'that' 'same' 'year.'

Tokens accordingly.

Continue etc.

But we actually need just the spans of named entities. The spans defined as indices from 0 to N-1? Or from 1? In example, tokens: ['I', ''', 'm', 'Radek', 'Stulc', ',', 'I', 'was', 'born', 'in', 'Prague', ',', 'and', 'I', 'am', 'currently', 'studying', 'at', 'CTU', '.']

Indices: 1: I, 2:''', 3:m, 4:Radek, 5:Stulc, 6:, etc. They output 'Radek Stulc - PERSON - 4-5' meaning start index 4, end index 5. So indices starting from 1? Because token 'Radek' is at index 4 indeed. Counting starts at 1. So spans are inclusive ranges start-end with 1-indexed token positions.

We need to compute token indices. Let's produce token list and index.

Let's write whole token list step by step.

I'll produce tokens and keep count.

I'll go line by line.

Full text: (Let's copy)

"Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1]), also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2] was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378. He was elected King of Germany (King of the Romans) in 1346 and became King of Bohemia (as Charles I) that same year. He was a member of the House of Luxembourg from his father's side and the Bohemian House of Přemyslid from his mother's side; he emphasized the latter due to his lifelong affinity for the Bohemian side of his inheritance, and also because his direct ancestors in the Přemyslid line included two saints.[3][4]

He was the eldest son and heir of John of Bohemia, King of Bohemia and Count of Luxembourg, who died at the Battle of Crécy on 26 August 1346. His mother, Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia, was the sister of Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland, the last of the male Přemyslid rulers of Bohemia. Charles inherited the County of Luxembourg from his father and was elected king of the Kingdom of Bohemia. On 2 September 1347, Charles was crowned King of Bohemia."

We'll treat punctuation as separate tokens. For example, '(' as token, ',' etc. Apostrophes separate like 's' into '’' or "'". We need to pay attention to characters like ’ (right single quote) and '−' dash.
